name: Shrivatsa Mark
description: The Shrivatsa Mark is a sacred symbol in Hinduism, particularly associated with Vishnu and his avatars, especially Krishna. It is a curl of hair on the chest of Vishnu, often depicted as a white lotus flower or an endless knot. It is considered a highly auspicious symbol, representing prosperity, beauty, and divine grace. The Shrivatsa is believed to be the permanent abode of Lakshmi, the goddess of wealth and fortune, signifying Vishnu's eternal connection with her. Its presence on Vishnu's chest symbolizes his compassionate heart and his role as the preserver of the universe. The mark is a reminder of the divine couple's inseparable nature and their combined power to bestow blessings upon devotees. Depictions of Vishnu, particularly in sculptural form, often feature the Shrivatsa prominently, emphasizing his divine nature and auspicious qualities. The symbol is not merely a physical mark but a representation of Vishnu's inherent divinity and his role in maintaining cosmic order. It serves as a focal point for devotion and contemplation, reminding devotees of the ultimate reality and the path to liberation. The absence of the Shrivatsa in depictions would be considered inauspicious, as it is integral to Vishnu's identity. It is an eternal sign of the presence of Lakshmi and the blessings of Vishnu. It is a powerful symbol representing Vishnu's compassion, Lakshmi's prosperity and the eternal bond between them. The placement of the Shrivatsa over Vishnu's heart signifies his love for all beings.
subtitle: Auspicious mark of Vishnu and Lakshmi
origin: Manifested on Vishnu's chest as the abode of Lakshmi.
currentLocation: Present on depictions and idols of Vishnu.

keyMyths.title: Lakshmi's Abode
keyMyths.description: The Shrivatsa is believed to be the permanent dwelling place of Lakshmi on Vishnu's chest, representing their eternal union and the inseparable nature of prosperity and preservation. This symbolizes that prosperity and wellbeing are always associated with the divine.

keyMyths.title: Mark of Divinity
keyMyths.description: The Shrivatsa mark distinguishes Vishnu and his avatars, like Krishna, as divine beings, indicating their role in maintaining cosmic order and bestowing blessings upon the world. It serves as a visual identifier of the deity's presence.

sources.author: Vettam Mani
sources.description: Comprehensive reference to Hindu mythology and iconography.
sources.text: Comprehensive reference to Hindu mythology and iconography.
